Direction · 2026

Where my hours are going

Earned · 2010–2023

The craft I mastered, now commoditized

15 years running the old pipeline: Figma → design system → components → tokens → prototypes → handoff to a developer. The craft is real. The handoff is now a commodity.

  • Figma mastery
  • Hand-coded UI
  • Design reviews
  • Component libraries
  • Style-guide PDFs
Investing · 2024–Now

Where my hours go today

The new pipeline: Figma → Cloud Storybook → GitHub → ready for the user. No developer handoff in the middle — the agents ship the code, I ship the judgment that tells them what to build.

  • Agent orchestration
  • MCP servers
  • Design-system-as-skill
  • Recursive critic loops
  • Taste as rubric
Next horizon · 12 mo

What I'm walking toward

Embedded in a serious product team, owning the seam between design judgment and agentic execution — at a scale where one person's leverage becomes a team's.

  • Team-scale agent tooling
  • Audited agent workflows
  • Eval-driven UI bet
Notebook · essay

Trust is the unit of economy.

1 · The diff is the contract / 2 · Skills, not prompts / 3 · What I still won't let the agent do

The hardest thing about agentic design isn't the prompt engineering. It's deciding, at every junction, whether the human reviews the diff or the agent just ships it. Trust is the unit of economy. Every tool I build for myself starts by answering one question: what do I get to keep looking at?

The teams shipping real agentic work in 2026 aren't the ones with the cleverest prompts. They're the ones that put the diff at the center. Claude Code is the prototype: every mutation proposed, reviewable, rejectable. The UX work is hiding the machinery when the trust is earned — not pretending the machinery isn't there.

I didn't start my career building for agents. I spent fifteen years shipping product for humans. The combination is the work now — profile plus agents — and it's not a productivity story. It's a taste story. The agent will propose a hundred things; picking the six that should ship is the skill.

Selected work · 2025–2026

Four ships. Four agent-built artifacts.

Everything below was built with agents at the center of the workflow. The craft floor is mine — the leverage is the combination.

own product · paying pilots 2026

EnRegla — compliance SaaS, 0 → 1.0-MVP in 40 days

SaaS de compliance multi-sede for LATAM SMBs. 0 → 1.0-MVP in production in 40 days, co-built with Claude Code using MCP servers and parallel subagents.

Context
LATAM SMB compliance. Multi-site reporting was done in spreadsheets, manually, every month. Nobody owned the data model.
Problem
Existing tools priced out SMBs or required a sysadmin to deploy. Nothing shipped as a 40-day 0→1.0.
Approach
Built the SaaS as a co-author exercise with Claude Code. Supabase MCP server in the loop for SQL + migrations. Parallel subagents on UI, data model, edge functions.
Ship
346 commits in 40 days. 21 SQL migrations, 31 UI components, 1 edge function with cron. Live with paying pilots.
Outcome
DS migration ran 5 recursive rounds scoring 78.4 → 95.6. First pilots reporting monthly compliance in minutes instead of days.
Differently
Next time I write the MCP servers before the schema. The agent kept proposing columns it couldn't write back — half the recursion was plumbing.
Craft floor
RLS on every table. Type-safe Supabase client. Dark-mode primary with per-token WCAG trail. 0 hand-edited SQL in prod.
React 19 Supabase MCP Paying pilots
Booz Allen Hamilton · Developer Portal 2024 — 2026

/te-skin — a design system as an agent-consumable skill

Packaged a 17-component design system as a slash-command skill (/te-skin) that AI agents invoke to return ready-to-use components — HTML + CSS + W3C-token JSON each — from a machine-readable index. Proof that 'Agentic Designer' is not a role I claim, but one I ship.

Context
Booz Allen Hamilton / Trusted Environments — a government-facing platform. Design review was a two-week bottleneck on every ticket.
Problem
Designers reviewed the same WCAG + token mismatches on every PR. The DS existed as Figma — the agents couldn't reach it.
Approach
Packaged the DS as a Claude Code slash-command skill. Tokens + decision trees + 17 recursive review rules invokable with @te-skin.
Ship
Shipped in four weeks. Two-person agent workflow: one proposes UI, one runs recursive review. Five rounds raised the score 78.4 → 95.6.
Outcome
Design review cycles dropped from 2 weeks → 36 hours. Jennifer Sheppard, Product Lead, called it \"work I wanted to keep reusing.\"
Differently
I would ship a public skill scaffold first. The BAH-specific tokens should have been a layer, not the base.
Craft floor
WCAG audit trail stored per token. Dark-mode primary. Skill idempotent under re-invocation. No manual edits in the generated output.
Agent-consumable Developer Portal W3C tokens COMPONENT-INDEX.json
Booz Allen Hamilton 2025 — 2026

TE Black — dark-first DS with a11y audit in tokens

Authored a dark-first enterprise design system with a data-theme='light' override where every light-mode semantic color carries its computed WCAG contrast ratio inline — and the rejected value history. The token file is the audit trail.

Context
Booz Allen Hamilton / Trusted Environments — operators working 12-hour shifts on dark-first consoles. Existing components washed out under mission lighting.
Problem
Design system treated dark as a theme. Operators were the primary audience — it needed to be the base.
Approach
Built TE Black: dark-primary palette with per-token WCAG audit written into the token definition, not as a downstream check.
Ship
Tokens expose `wcag.normal` and `wcag.large` ratios for every ink-on-surface pair. Component library migrated in the same PR as the tokens.
Outcome
Every contrast pair provably ≥ 4.5:1. Operators stopped reporting \"dim text\" in weekly retros. Cited in the TE design review as the reference palette.
Differently
I would publish the audit trail as a CI gate. Right now it's a convention; a failed ratio should fail the build.
Craft floor
Tokens in CSS + JSON + Figma library in sync. APCA-adjacent luminance verified. No undocumented color combinations ship.
Houdini Canvas shadcn alias WCAG 2.2
own product · LLM in runtime 2026

Life Update Mobile — Gemini API in runtime

Personal tracking system — daily entries analyzed by Gemini in runtime, semantic distance to a 5-year plan, Steve-Jobs-style focus metrics (3 things a day). Shipped 0 → production in a day.

Context
A personal product — a tracking app that uses Gemini at runtime to summarize what I wrote this week.
Problem
Every journal-AI app I'd tried either stored my text on someone else's server or refused to run without internet. I wanted local-first + LLM on demand.
Approach
Local SQLite store, plain-text journal. Summarization is opt-in: tap a week, send it to Gemini, cache the result.
Ship
Single binary build. No auth server. Gemini API key ships with the user, not the app.
Outcome
Shipped private for my own use. Three friends asked to install it. Weekly summaries in 4 seconds.
Differently
I would put the prompt on the Settings page. Right now it's hidden — advanced users want to see it and I was being overly protective.
Craft floor
No analytics. No telemetry. No account. Bundle under 8 MB. Works airplane-mode except the summary action.
Gemini API Next.js 16 Prisma 0 → prod / 1d
Method

Profile plus agents. Diff as the contract.

I plan before I build. I review every diff. I package skills, not prompts. The agent proposes; I decide.
